name: Italian Whole Wheat Focaccia Crackers
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon olive oil for the tops
recipeInstructions:
"This is an adaptation of Italian focaccia. It is one of the most crispy and crunchy crackers, as well as one of the most substantial. Try serving Focaccia Crackers with soft cheese or garlic-flavored cream cheese. It is somewhat more difficult to make than most other crackers, but worth twice the trouble.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
20 to 30 minutes
In a small bowl, combine yeast and sugar with 1/4 cup warm water. Set aside in a warm place until the mixture starts to foam, about 5 to 10 minutes.
Combine flours and salt in a large bowl or food processor. Cut shortening until mixture resembles coarse meal. Stir in yeast mixture and remaining 1/4 cup warm water. Blend into dough that holds together in a cohesive ball.
Knead by hand for about 10 minutes on a lightly floured surface, or pulse about 25 seconds in food processor. Knead until dough is smooth and springy to the touch.
Place dough in lightly oiled bowl and turn over to coat all sides. Cover with damp towel and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 1-1/2 to 2 hours.
Punch down dough and turn out onto a lightly floured surface or pastry cloth. Roll into rectangular shape about 1/4 inch thick. Place on lightly greased or parchment-lined baking sheet, cover with damp towel, and let rise another 35 to 45 minutes.
Using fingers or soft pastry brush, gently press fingertips all over top of risen dough, leaving indentations. Cover again and let double in bulk for another 1-1/2 to 2 hours.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Brush tops lightly with olive oil. Bake 20 to 30 minutes until very crisp. Cool on rack. Break into small irregular pieces or cut into 2 large crackers that can be broken as they are nibbled.
VARIATIONS: Sprinkle dough with rosemary, basil, or favorite herb after brushing with olive oil."
